data.content:
Here's what I see in the crash log:

**Crash Summary:**
- **App**: `com.companyname.MauiTest` (.NET MAUI on Android arm64)
- **Signal**: SIGABRT — abort message: `"not compatible"`
- **Memory hint**: UTF-16 string `"some intentional crash on startup."`

**.NET Runtime Frames** (all in `libmonosgen-2.0.so`):
| Frame | PC Offset |
|-------|-----------|
| #01 | `0x222098` |
| #02 | `0x2241ac` |
| #03 | `0x1abc80` |
| #04 | `0x1abd14` |
| #05 | `0x16dfa4` |
| #06 | `0x16db6c` |
| #07 | `0x16de04` |
| #08 | JIT'd managed code (anonymous memory) |

**BuildId**: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`

Let me try to fetch debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server and symbolicate these frames.

arguments.command:
# Check available tools
which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>&1; echo "---"; dpkg -l | grep -i binutils 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try to download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# ELF BuildId format: https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/elf-buildid-<buildid>/<filename>
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-7a9f9225dcd44f48aef56f66c24e42bb/symbols"
mkdir -p "$SYMDIR"

# Try the Microsoft symbol server with ELF buildid convention
URL="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"
echo "Trying: $URL"
curl -sfSL -o "$SYMDIR/${FILENAME}.dbg" "$URL" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1

# Also try the sym variant
URL2="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"
echo "Trying: $URL2"
curl -sfSL -o "$SYMDIR/${FILENAME}.sym" "$URL2" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

data.reasoningText:
This is an Android tombstone/crash log (not an Apple .ips file), so the apple-crash-symbolication skill is not appropriate here.

Let me analyze the crash:

1. **Platform**: Android (arm64)
2. **App**: `com.companyname.MauiTest` - a .NET MAUI app
3. **Signal**: SIGABRT with abort message "not compatible"
4. **Runtime**: `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (Mono runtime, used by .NET MAUI on Android)
5. **BuildId**: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`


6. **Crash frames**: The .NET runtime frames (#01-#07) are all in `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, with frame #08 likely being JIT-compiled managed code in anonymous memory

To symbolicate this, I need ELF debug symbols for the Mono runtime library—either embedded DWARF data or a separate `.dbg` file. Microsoft publishes .NET runtime symbols for Android, and the BuildId should help locate the correct version.

I can try downloading the symbols from Microsoft's symbol server using the ELF BuildId, then use tools like `addr2line` or `llvm-symbolizer` to resolve the addresses to function names and source locations.

arguments.command:
# Check available tools
which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>&1; echo "---"; dpkg -l | grep -i binutils 2>/dev/null | head -5

# Try to download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# ELF BuildId format: https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/elf-buildid-<buildid>/<filename>
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-7a9f9225dcd44f48aef56f66c24e42bb/symbols"
mkdir -p "$SYMDIR"

# Try the Microsoft symbol server with ELF buildid convention
URL="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"
echo "Trying: $URL"
curl -sfSL -o "$SYMDIR/${FILENAME}.dbg" "$URL" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1

# Also try the sym variant
URL2="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"
echo "Trying: $URL2"
curl -sfSL -o "$SYMDIR/${FILENAME}.sym" "$URL2" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "# Check available tools\nwhich addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>&1; echo \"---\"; dpkg -l | grep -i binutils 2>/dev/null | head -5", "description": "Check for symbolication tools", "initial_wait": 10}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args:
{"command": "# Try to download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# ELF BuildId format: https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/elf-buildid-<buildid>/<filename>
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-7a9f9225dcd44f48aef56f66c24e42bb/symbols"
mkdir -p "$SYMDIR"

# Try the Microsoft symbol server with ELF buildid convention
URL="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"
echo "Trying: $URL"
curl -sfSL -o "$SYMDIR/${FILENAME}.dbg" "$URL" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1

# Also try the sym variant
URL2="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"
echo "Trying: $URL2"
curl -sfSL -o "$SYMDIR/${FILENAME}.sym" "$URL2" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1", "description": "Download debug symbols from MS symbol server", "initial_wait": 30}

toolArgs.command:
# Check available tools
which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>&1; echo "---"; dpkg -l | grep -i binutils 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolResult.textResultForLlm:

toolArgs.command:
# Try to download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# ELF BuildId format: https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/elf-buildid-<buildid>/<filename>
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-7a9f9225dcd44f48aef56f66c24e42bb/symbols"
mkdir -p "$SYMDIR"

# Try the Microsoft symbol server with ELF buildid convention
URL="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"
echo "Trying: $URL"
curl -sfSL -o "$SYMDIR/${FILENAME}.dbg" "$URL" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1

# Also try the sym variant
URL2="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"
echo "Trying: $URL2"
curl -sfSL -o "$SYMDIR/${FILENAME}.sym" "$URL2" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1
toolArgs.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

arguments.command:
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-7a9f9225dcd44f48aef56f66c24e42bb/symbols"
SYMFILE="$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg"

# Check what sections are available (symbol tables, debug info)
readelf -S "$SYMFILE" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '(symtab|dynsym|debug|strtab)' | head -20
echo "=== Sections with debug ==="
readelf -S "$SYMFILE" 2>/dev/null | grep -i debug
echo "=== Symbol count ==="
readelf --dyn-syms "$SYMFILE" 2>/dev/null | wc -l
echo "=== First few dynamic symbols ==="
readelf --dyn-syms "$SYMFILE" 2>/dev/null | head -10

data.reasoningText:
The file is an ELF 64-bit ARM aarch64 shared object, but it says "stripped". This means it has the code but no debug symbols (DWARF info). However, it should still have the symbol table (function names via .dynsym). Let me check if there are symbols available and try to use addr2line or llvm-symbolizer.

Wait, but addr2line on an x86_64 system won't work for aarch64 binaries with standard binutils. Let me check if we have aarch64 cross tools, or use llvm-symbolizer which is architecture-agnostic for reading DWARF.


Actually, llvm-symbolizer should work since it just reads the DWARF/symbol table regardless of architecture. Even though the file is stripped of debug symbols, there might still be a .dynsym section with function names. Let me use readelf or nm to check—those tools can read aarch64 ELF files even from an x86_64 system.
